key_strengths: Two-molecule, two-disease franchise — among the most understandable in global pharma
Unrivaled peptide manufacturing scale (14 sites, DKK 90B FY2025 capex) — 5–7 year replication barrier
Elite economics: 81% gross margin, 41% operating margin, 36% ROIC, DKK 102B owner earnings
Aa3/AA balance sheet — 0.6x Net Debt/EBITDA, 32x interest coverage, DKK 15B buyback restarted
Foundation governance with 77% voting control and multi-century horizon
Q1 2026 oral Wegovy at 2× consensus, Wegovy 65% of US new prescriptions — first inflection evidence
FDA April 30 2026 proposal closes 503B compounded-GLP-1 leakage channel
5.5% dividend yield pays you to wait through the capex cycle
key_risks: Lilly retatrutide 2026 filing may land with breakout efficacy (>26% weight loss)
US net pricing structurally reset — IRA $274 (2027) and MFN $245/month locked in
Single-mechanism (GLP-1 + amylin) concentration — pipeline extends, doesn't diversify
Lilly tirzepatide holds ~60% US total GLP-1 share; efficacy crown lost until amycretin reads out
Capex cycle wisdom unresolved until 2027–2029; reported FCF will look weak in interim
Composition-of-matter patents on semaglutide expire early 2030s
25–30% drawdown risk plausible on retatrutide data or CagriSema CRL
red_flags: FY2026 guidance still implies revenue decline of −4% to −12% — first down year in nearly a decade
Gross margin compressed 370bp in a single year (84.7% → 81.0%) — structural, not mix
Total debt rose 5× (DKK 25.8B → DKK 131B) in two fiscal years at peak earnings
Buybacks collapsed from DKK 30B (FY2023) to DKK 1.4B (FY2025) at exactly the moment shares became cheap
Executive direct ownership effectively zero; 2025 insider transactions were net sales
Dual-class voting permanently disenfranchises minority B-shareholders
CagriSema underperformed Zepbound in Novo's own head-to-head study
buy_triggers: Pullback to DKK 230–260 on retatrutide noise without thesis change — scale starter to 4–5%
Q2/Q3 2026 oral Wegovy script trend stays >65% US new prescriptions with pill revenue ≥ DKK 2B/quarter
CagriSema FDA approval (expected late 2026) with clean label
sell_triggers: Lilly retatrutide ships best-in-class (>26% weight loss + clean safety) AND amycretin Phase 3 disappoints
Class-wide semaglutide safety signal forcing label restriction or withdrawal
ROIC below 15% for two consecutive years — permanent capex impairment
Re-rate above DKK 600 (~20x P/E on flat earnings) without commensurate fundamental improvement — trim
Foundation governance changes materially (dual-class collapse or hostile board change)
valuation_summary: Analyst-adjusted IV range DKK 460 / 690 / 950 (bear/base/bull) with probability-weighted central estimate DKK 525 vs current price DKK 300, giving a 35% MOS to conservative and ~75% upside to probability-weighted central. Adjustments lift WACC to 7%, fade margins to 36–38%, and place 30% weight on a decline scenario that the quant model does not sample.
compact_checklist: Business: Novo Nordisk sells semaglutide-based GLP-1 drugs (Ozempic, Wegovy injectable and oral) plus a legacy insulin franchise to chronic diabetes and obesity patients globally, with Q1 2026 revenue of DKK 96.8B (+32% CER) and oral Wegovy already at 65% of US new prescriptions in its first full quarter.

Moat: A peptide manufacturing footprint of 14 sites built with DKK 90B of FY2025 capex (Catalent, Kalundborg, Clayton, Chartres) plus 154 granted semaglutide patents extending to 2042 — a barrier that would cost a competitor $15–25B and 5–7 years to replicate, even though Eli Lilly's tirzepatide currently holds ~60% of US total GLP-1 share.

Economics: FY2025 produced 81% gross margin, 41% operating margin, 36% ROIC and DKK 102B of owner earnings (reported FCF of DKK 29B is a capex-cycle artifact, not an earnings problem) — elite by any pharma standard, even after a structural reset from the freakish 70% ROIC of FY2023.

Management: New CEO Maziar Mike Doustdar (in role since August 2025) cut 9,000 jobs for DKK 8B in run-rate savings, restarted a DKK 15B buyback, and delivered a Q1 2026 beat plus guidance raise — all under the permanent stewardship of the Novo Nordisk Foundation, which controls 28% of equity and 77% of votes on a multi-century time horizon.

Debt risk: With Net Debt/EBITDA of 0.6x, interest coverage of 32x, Aa3/AA credit ratings (among the highest in global pharma), and DKK 119B of annual operating cash flow, Novo would survive a 30% revenue decline with coverage still above 12x — financial distress is not a realistic scenario.

Price: At DKK 300 the market is pricing roughly the "decline scenario" (IV ~DKK 240 — flat-to-negative revenue for 5 years, margins compressing to 30%, exit multiple below 10x EV/EBITDA), so today's price only makes sense if you believe Lilly's retatrutide takes another 10+ points of share AND US MFN pricing keeps ratcheting down AND oral generics arrive before 2030.

Margin of safety: 35% discount to my conservative IV of DKK 460 (43% to my probability-weighted DKK 525) plus a 5.5% dividend yield that pays you to wait, on top of a business with 81% gross margins and a balance sheet that can't be broken — even a re-test to the 52-week low of DKK 220 leaves an upside-to-downside ratio of roughly 2.8x.

Thesis breaker: Sell if Lilly's retatrutide ships with clearly best-in-class efficacy (>26% weight loss with clean safety) AND Novo's amycretin disappoints in Phase 3 (2027–2028), OR if semaglutide faces a class-wide safety signal forcing label restriction, OR if ROIC falls below 15% for two consecutive years signaling permanent capex impairment.
source_summary: Auto-fetched yfinance financials (2026-05-11), src/quant DCF model output, Novo Nordisk Q1 2026 release (May 6, 2026) and investor materials, Annual Report 2025, Moody's Aa3 and S&P AA credit opinions, FDA April 30 2026 503B bulks-list proposal, CNBC/Reuters/GlobeNewswire/BioPharma Dive/FiercePharma coverage, prior week15 NOVO-B.CO continuity report. Data is current as of analysis date; primary staleness caveat is that retatrutide Phase 3 obesity readout and CagriSema FDA decision (both late-2026 expected) will materially update the competitive picture.
